title: Calathinus serotinus
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the specific plant "Calathinus serotinus." However, I found information on the plant "Calendula serotina", but most importantly the "Calendula serotina" is a synonym of Calendula officinalis's subsp "serotina"  and it is a synonym of  Calendula arvensis  but most importantly, Calendula arvensis is a synonym of Calendula serotina  and another synonym of  Calendula officinalis.

Calendula officinalis, often referred to as Calathinus serotinus (the name that seems to be incorrect) is a plant that belongs to the daisy family and is commonly known as pot marigold. The health benefits of Calendula officinalis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which make it useful for wound healing, skin conditions, and soothing digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Calendula officinalis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as ointments, creams, and teas, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
